articles.title: jQuery 4.0 Released
articles.description: 20 years on from its original release, the ever-popular (in terms of actual usage) library reaches 4.0 with a migration to ES modules (compatible with modern build tools) along with dropping support for IE 10 and older. With jQuery being a popular guest in our newsletters in the early years, it’s fantastic to see it pop back for a quick visit.
articles.author: Timmy Willison

articles.title: Astro is Joining Cloudflare
articles.description: Big news in the Web framework space as the team behind the popular Astro framework (the beta of v6.0 is now available) is headed to Cloudflare. Few major frameworks are now not under the wing of a larger entity.
articles.author: Schott and Irvine-Broque

articles.title: ASCII Characters Are Not Pixels: A Deep Dive Into ASCII Rendering
articles.description: Alex digs deep into getting ASCII-based graphics rendering just right with JavaScript, complete with examples of the algorithms used and numerous demos. The neatest technical blog post I’ve seen so far this year.
articles.author: Alex Harri

articles.title: JavaScript Now a First-Class Citizen in Aspire
articles.description: Aspire is a Microsoft framework for orchestrating the deployment of distributed apps. Originally just for .NET, Aspire 13 now makes JavaScript a first-class citizen, so you can run Vite and full-stack JS apps with service discovery, telemetry, and production-ready containers.
articles.author: Microsoft

articles.title: Introducing the <geolocation> Element
articles.description: Chrome 144 introduces a new <geolocation> element for requesting user location data, moving away from a JavaScript-triggered prompt.
articles.author: Viana, Le, Steiner

articles.title: Starry Night 3.9: GitHub-Like Syntax Highlighting
articles.description: GitHub’s own syntax highlighter isn’t open source, but this library is a powerful alternative that tries to get as close as it can, with support for hundreds of languages. I’ve put a basic Web demo here to show off how to use it on the Web.
articles.author: Titus Wormer

articles.title: Extension.js 3: Browser Extension Development Framework
articles.description: Create cross-browser extensions without manual build configuration and develop, build, and preview across browsers with a unified workflow. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Cezar Augusto et al.

articles.title: React Aria: Adobe's World-Class React Components
articles.description: React Aria has a fantastic new site and all-new documentation that really sells the entire experience, complete with interactive CSS and Tailwind examples to get started quickly.
articles.author: Adobe

articles.title: localspace: Modern localForage-Compatible Storage Toolkit
articles.description: localForage is/was a popular storage library that wrapped various browser storage APIs with a simple, localStorage-like API. It hasn’t been updated for years, though, and “localspace exists to bridge that gap”.
articles.author: Michael Lin

articles.description: 🕒 Temporal Playground is an online sandbox for playing around with the Temporal API.
Svelte has released patches for five vulnerabilities affecting the Svelte ecosystem.

🤖 Ryan Dahl, creator of both Node.js and Deno, says on X that "the era of humans writing code is over" and "That's not to say SWEs don't have work to do, but writing syntax directly is not it." I hope not, but these are interesting times!
Electron 40.0 – The popular cross-platform desktop app framework upgrades to Chromium 144, V8 14.4, and Node 24.11.1.
Node.js v25.4.0 (Current) – require(esm) is now marked as stable.
React Native Windows 0.81, Aurelia 2 RC, Deno 2.6.5

articles.title: The 2025 Web Almanac
articles.description: This always interesting, detailed, annual report returns, having analyzed over 17 million websites and processed some 244TB of data! It highlights how the web has evolved over the past year — told via chapters on accessibility, performance, security, content distribution, AI and more. “We hope our report helps the community understand the current state of the web better”.
articles.author: HTTP Archive

articles.title: jQuery 4.0 Released
articles.description: This major release comes 20 years on from the library's debut! Version 4.0 drops support for IE 10 and older, adds in support for Trusted Types, aligns with the W3C spec on focus events, and most notably sees a full migration to ES modules — meaning it’s compatible with modern build tools!
articles.author: Timmy Willison

articles.title: Astro is Joining Cloudflare
articles.description: The team behind popular framework Astro are joining Cloudflare. This move means that most of the major web frameworks are now under the wing of a large company. Related: (The beta of v6.0 is now available).
articles.author: Schott and Irvine-Broque

articles.title: Building docfind: Fast Client-Side Search with Rust and WebAssembly
articles.description: An interesting case study from the VS Code team about how they added a new, performant search system (powered by WebAssembly) to their growing site. The engine powering it is docfind — the repo for which is here — there’s also a demo where you can see just how fast it is in serving results from an example index of 50,000 news articles. Speedy!
articles.author: João Moreno

articles.title: Rethinking “Pixel Perfect” Web Design
articles.description: “clinging to the idea of ‘pixel perfection’ isn’t just anachronistic, it’s actively harmful to the products we build”.
articles.author: Amit Sheen

articles.title: Fix Your robots.txt or Your Site Can Disappear from Google
articles.description: An FYI that it's possible sites can be removed from Google search results if they don’t have a valid robots.txt file or, critically, if Googlebot can’t access it.
articles.author: alan w smith

articles.title: How to Detect Safari and iOS Versions from JavaScript
articles.description: Naturally, progressive enhancement is the best policy, but if you need to..
articles.author: Evgeniy Valyaev

articles.title: ASCII Characters Are Not Pixels: A Deep Dive Into ASCII Rendering
articles.description: A really neat post that digs in to technicalities of rendering ASCII-based graphics with JavaScript. Lots of great examples and live demos here.
articles.author: Alex Harri

articles.title: Touch Targets and Web Accessibility
articles.description: A good, quick primer on ensuring your touch targets are big enough.
articles.author: Ilknur Eren

articles.title: Better SVG: VS Code Extension for Editing SVG Files with Live Preview
articles.description: Lots of useful features including live preview, color picker, zoom/pan controls, editable currentColor value for color schemes, dark background toggle, and SVGO integration.
articles.author: Miguel Ángel Durán García

articles.title: Stylelint 17.0: A 'Mighty' Modern CSS Linter
articles.description: An established linter with over 100 built-in rules to help “catch errors, apply limits and enforce stylistic conventions”. v17 has a special migration guide due to numerous breaking changes and updates. GitHub repo.
articles.author: stylelint

articles.title: Extension.js 3: Cross Browser Extension Development Framework
articles.description: Create cross-browser extensions without manual build configuration and develop, build, and preview across browsers with a unified workflow. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Cezar Augusto et al.

articles.title: Styleframe: TypeScript CSS API for Writing Design System Code
articles.description: Among other features, it’s framework-agnostic, has built-in theme management, and allows you to build out your system using a composable and easy to configure API. Repo here.
articles.author: Styleframe

articles.title: HTML Minifier Next: A Powerful JavaScript-Based HTML Minifier
articles.description: A CLI-based HTML minifier tool that can also handle in-document CSS, JavaScript, and SVG minification. Jens shares an update on the project here.
articles.author: Jens Oliver Meiert

articles.title: DiffMaster: A Tool to Compare JSON and YAML Config Files
articles.description: Outputs a searchable, exportable diff summary and the app will store your last 10 comparisons locally. Repo.
articles.author: Alexandre

articles.title: localspace: Modern localForage-Compatible Storage Toolkit
articles.description: localForage was a popular storage library that wrapped various browser storage APIs with a simple, localStorage-like API. It hasn’t been updated for years, though, and “localspace exists to bridge that gap”.
articles.author: Michael Lin

articles.title: 52 Optical Illusions Created in CSS
articles.description: Alvaro has put together an impressive collection of various optical illusions, all of which are using CSS to great effect. Some really neat stuff here.
articles.author: Alvaro Montoro
